body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** Internal engineering epic to migrate the **Azure Storage File** package from AutoRest to TypeSpec-based code generation — a toolchain modernization with no user-visible API changes.

The issue is created by a repo member (`lmazuel`) and is already assigned to `@jorgerangel-msft`. It is clearly scoped, has guiding principles, a spec branch reference, and an internal Loop page for progress tracking.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Internal Epic / Task |
| **Service** | Azure Storage (File Storage) |
| **SDK** | `Azure.Storage.Files.Shares` (client library under `/sdk/storage`) |
| **Goal** | Migrate from AutoRest → TypeSpec code generation |
| **Breaking Changes** | None allowed — APIView should be unchanged |
| **Test Impact** | No test changes or re-recording required (regression test of the migration itself) |
| **Spec Branch** | `feature/file-tsp` on `azure-rest-api-specs` |

**Note:** The issue body says "Storage Blobs package" but the title says "Storage File" — the spec branch points at `FileStorage`, so the target library is File Storage, not Blobs. Worth clarifying if both are in scope or if the body description is a copy-paste artifact.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔑 Key Constraints & Principles</summary>

As stated in the issue:
- **Toolchain migration only** — no improvements to the convenience layer
- **No breaking changes** — APIView diff should be empty
- **No test folder changes** — existing tests serve as regression coverage
- Manual paging logic in Storage stays as-is (TypeSpec paging for Storage is out of scope)
- Easy TypeSpec updates are acceptable if timeline impact is minimal
- `tsp-location.yaml` should target the `feature/file-tsp` branch for now

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Suggested Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Confirm scope: File Storage only, or also Blobs? (body vs. title discrepancy)
- [ ] Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `feature/file-tsp` branch in `azure-rest-api-specs`
- [ ] Validate TypeSpec spec at `specification/storage/data-plane/FileStorage` on the feature branch
- [ ] Run `tsp-client update` to generate the SDK from the TypeSpec spec
- [ ] Build the generated code — resolve any compilation errors
- [ ] Run `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 storage` and confirm **zero diff** in the API listing (no breaking changes)
- [ ] Run existing tests (no re-recording) to confirm behavioral parity
- [ ] Update `tsp-location.yaml` to target main spec once the spec branch is merged
- [ ] Open a PR and request APIView comparison

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Useful Resources</summary>

- [TypeSpec local SDK workflow instructions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/instructions/azsdk-tools/local-sdk-workflow.instructions.md)
- [azure-rest-api-specs feature/file-tsp branch — FileStorage spec](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/feature/file-tsp/specification/storage/data-plane/FileStorage)
- [sdk-migration skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/copilot-instructions.md) — covers AutoRest→TypeSpec migration patterns
- Related open issue: [#54775 — MPG migration for Storage](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/54775) (management plane, different scope)
- [TypeSpec client generator CLI docs](https://github.com/Azure/typespec-azure)

</details>

<details>
<summary>⚠️ Potential Pitfalls</summary>

- **Paging**: Storage has custom manual paging — ensure the TypeSpec emitter does not attempt to generate paging code that conflicts with existing hand-written implementations.
- **API surface**: Even minor renames (e.g., casing differences in model properties) would be breaking. Run `Export-API.ps1` early and often.
- **Serialization quirks**: Storage REST APIs have unique XML serialization patterns (e.g., for list responses). Verify the TypeSpec spec captures these correctly.
- **Feature branch drift**: The spec is on a feature branch — keep `tsp-location.yaml` locked to that branch until the spec is merged to main.

</details>
